title: Update rootfs.md: Fix formatting and wording of idmap option
body:
The newly introduced `idmap` section of rootfs lacked a header (comparable to Overlay Rootfs Mounts), had odd formatting, and wording that differed from other instances of idmap, e.g., the one in the --volume section. This commits addresses those issues.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: @vrothberg This is the new PR, the one I think I did wrong. Any ideas on best way forward? I will apply what I learn here to the other doc update I have waiting in the wings.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *PeterWhittaker*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> @vrothberg This is the new PR, the one I think I did wrong. Any ideas on best way forward? I will apply what I learn here to the other doc update I have waiting in the wings.

To me all looks in order here. Do you see an issue?

> To me all looks in order here. Do you see an issue?

Not with the content, but with how I submitted the PR; I think I missed the release notes step or something. Since it looks like things are moving forward, I will wait for this one to be done, then push my other doc change and submit a PR for that, tagging you. Thanks!

> I think I missed the release notes step or something

Thanks for pointing that out.  You can edit the initial message of this pull request and either add a release not to `Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?` or just add `None`.

> Thanks for pointing that out. You can edit the initial message of this pull request and either add a release not to `Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?` or just add `None`.

Ah, retroactive application! Done. :->

I find it funny that doc changes are not considered user-facing, though I understand the overall intent: Is this something we need to verify for user-visible-breakage, not is this something intended for users that few if any will ever read....

> I find it funny that doc changes are not considered user-facing, though I understand the overall intent: Is this something we need to verify for user-visible-breakage, not is this something intended for users that few if any will ever read....

That's how I understand the intention as well.  Feel free to add a text on "Fixing man pages for $subject".  I do not feel strongly about it.

This can also be done once the PR has merged.  Thanks again for contributing!

/lgtnm
